verdict_reasoning: CEACAM5 is explicitly described as a 'cell surface glycoprotein' in the NCBI summary. It is designated CD66e, confirming cell-surface antigen status. A founding member of the CEA/CEACAM family, it is GPI-anchored and mediates cell-adhesion and cellular signaling. The V-set and I-set domain memberships further confirm extracellular topology. The protein is clinically used as a biomarker, supporting direct surface accessibility for antibody engagement.
